title: Why Socialism by Albert Einstein
excerpt: Albert Einstein explains why he support socialism and thinks capitalism is evil.
content:
We all know Albert Einstein was a famous physicist, but most of us are unaware of the fact that he was actively involved in politics much of his life. He campaigned against war, advocated for socialism and opposed using nuclear weapons. He believed that scientists have a moral obligation towards humanity. In this short essay Einstein offers a moral critique of capitalism

“The economic anarchy of capitalism is in my opinion the real source of evil.”

While many economists have criticised his proposed solution as economically underdeveloped, a wide range of political philosophers acknowledge that he was morally right. Delighted to read something like this from an extraordinary thinker.

title: Shiah Muslims
excerpt: The term Shīʿah means “followers of Ali” and originally referred to those supporting ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib after the Prophet’s death. Over time, Shia Islam developed into several branches, with Twelver Shiʿism representing about 85% of Shia Muslims.
content:
The word shīʿah derived from شيعة علي, shīʿatu ʿAlī, ‘followers of Ali’. The term shīʿah itself means “party” or “faction,” and the term first appears with reference to those who followed ʿAlī in the wars that he fought as caliph against the Umayyads. [1] Shiah people are those who believe that Hazrat Ali and his successors are the only people who can be the Imams and they will lead the muslims.

There are many sects of Shiah muslims some of their believes are totally against islam. [2]

Twelverism (Imami): This is the largest brach of shiah muslim.[3] The percentage of Twelversim shiah is around 85%[4]. This writing is only focused on the majority of the Shiah muslims but there are many other groups and subgroups their believes can be different which is not the topic.

ড. খোন্দকার আব্দুল্লাহ জাহাঙ্গীর’s book “কুরআন সুন্নাহর আলোকে ইসলামী আকীদা” where he mentioned all the wrong belives of shia muslim. Most of the shiah (Twelver almost 85%) they believe that including there chalips Abu Bakr, Omar, Uthman ® all the companions of Harzat Muhammad (PBUH) are Munafiq, Murtad (Those who leaves Islam) and Oppressors. They also believe that they spreading hatred against all companions except the Ali’s family is a part of Iman. Furthermore, they don’t believe in those Hadiths narrated by companions. [5]

Some Fatwa From Sunni Islamic Scholars

In one recorded question and answer session with Sheikh Saleh bin Fawzan al-Fawzan, a current member of the Council of Senior Religious Scholars, a man asks whether it is permissible to call rafidha and Shia “our brothers.” The sheikh responds, “We are exonerated by God for this statement, they are not our brothers … rather they are brothers of Satan…. Whoever says they are our brothers must repent.” [6]

In September 2016, Saudi Arabia’s current grand mufti Abdulaziz al-Sheikh told the Okaz Newspaper that Iranians, who are mostly Shia, are not Muslims, stating “We must understand that they are not Muslims, they are sons of Majus (a pejorative term for Zoroastrian), and their hostility with Muslims is a longstanding matter, particularly with Sunnis.”[7]

One of the greatest islamic scholar of sunni muslim in 21th century Abdulaziz bin Baz, In responding to a question regarding the difference between the Sunni and the Shia, bin Baz noted that some Shia are unbelievers while others are not, but that “among the most evil of them are the Twelver Imams and Al-Nusayriyyah referring to the Alawi community who are also called Al-Rafidah." [6:1] And they are the largest brach of shia muslims. [4:1]

There was a incident where a six year old was killed just because of the fact that he is a shiah! Even though this is irrelevant but the way sunni muslim scholars spread hatred against them this kind thing can be occur[8]

  1. The New Encyclopædia Britannica, 15th ed., Encyclopædia Britannica, Inc., 1998, ISBN 0-85229-663-0, Vol. 10, p. 738 ↩︎

  2. https://islamqa.info/en/answers/97448/information-about-the-shiah ↩︎

  3. Esposito, John. What Everyone Needs to Know about Islam, Oxford University Press, 2002. ISBN 978-0-19-515713-0. p. 40 ↩︎

  4. Guidère, Mathieu (2012). Historical Dictionary of Islamic Fundamentalism. Scarecrow Press. p. 319. ISBN 978-0-8108-7965-2 – via Google Books. ↩︎ ↩︎

  5. ড. খোন্দকার আব্দুল্লাহ জাহাঙ্গীর, কুরআন সুন্নাহর আলোকে ইসলামী আকীদা (ঢাকা: আস-সুন্নাহ পাবলিকেশন্স, 2022), 686. ↩︎

  6. https://www.hrw.org/report/2017/09/26/they-are-not-our-brothers/hate-speech-saudi-officials ↩︎ ↩︎

  7. “Saudi Mufti Responds to Khamenei: You are not Muslims but sons of Majus hostile to Sunnis,” CNN, September 6, 2016, http://arabic.cnn.com/middleeast/2016/09/06/saudi-iran-mufti (accessed March 26, 2017). ↩︎

  8. https://shiarightswatch.org/a-six-years-old-child-was-beheaded-in-saudi-arabia/ ↩︎

title: Intro to Philosophy
excerpt: Albert Einstein explains why he support socialism and thinks capitalism is evil.
content:
What is philosophy and why should we care about this? Some people are averse to learn this subject but don’t understand the value of this. The word philosophy derives from Greek. The word is combination of two words; philo which means love and sophy it means knowledge, wisdom. If we combine the words which literally means that love of wisdom. [1] Philosophy is one of the foundation of all knowledge. It forces us to think critically and logically without any kind of bias. Most of use are bounded by our circumstances without noticing. Many of our believes are shaped by the society which we belongs to. But the nuances is that believing what the society is inherently bad or that can’t be true. Which can be or can’t be.

There are four main brunches of philosophy which are epistemology, ethics, logic, and metaphysics. Epistemology deals with what is knowledge and how to gain it. Ethics studies the moral goodness and wrongness of an act. Logic studies how to distinguish good arguments from the bad ones. And finally metaphysics try to explain the reality what is it, is there anything beyond this observable universe or not and so forth.

Before the modern age the term philosophy also used to describe the sciences. Natural philosophy aka science was a major branch of philosophy. [2] This brach of philosophy included a wide range of fields such as biology, chemistry, physics and so on. One example is Issac Newton’s book Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica which was a physics book but he named it Philosophiæ. [3]

  1. Hoad, T. F. (1993). The Concise Oxford Dictionary of English Etymology. Oxford University Press. ISBN 0-19-283098-8. ↩︎

  2. Cotterell, Brian (2017). Physics and Culture. World Scientific. ISBN 978-1-78634-378-9. Retrieved 25 August 2023. ↩︎

  3. Regenbogen, Arnim (2010). “Philosophiebegriffe”. In Sandkühler, Hans Jörg (ed.). Enzyklopädie Philosophie (in German). Meiner. Archived from the original on 20 March 2022. Retrieved 15 February 2022. ↩︎

What is philosophy and why should we care about this? Some people are averse to learn this subject but don't understand the value of this. The word philosophy derives from Greek. The word is combination of two words; ***philo*** which means love and ***sophy*** it means knowledge, wisdom. If we combine the words which literally means that ***love of wisdom***. [^1]
![Blog/Intro to Philosophy/attachments/islamqa.png](?blogintro-to-philosophyattachmentsislamqapng)
Philosophy is one of the foundation of all knowledge. It forces us to think critically and logically without any kind of bias. Most of use are bounded by our circumstances without noticing. Many of our believes are shaped by the society which we belongs to. But the nuances is that believing what the society is inherently bad or that can't be true. Which can be or can't be. 

There are four main brunches of philosophy which are [epistemology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epistemology), [ethics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethics "Ethics"), [logic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logic "Logic"), and [metaphysics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metaphysics "Metaphysics"). Epistemology deals with what is knowledge and how to gain it. Ethics studies the moral goodness and wrongness of an act. Logic studies how to distinguish good arguments from the bad ones. And finally metaphysics try to explain the reality what is it, is there anything beyond this observable universe or not and so forth. 

Before the modern age the term philosophy also used to describe the sciences. Natural philosophy aka science was a major branch of philosophy. [^2] This brach of philosophy included a wide range of fields such as biology, chemistry, physics and so on. One example is Issac Newton's book ***Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica*** which was a physics book but he named it Philosophiæ. [^3]
